commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a generated-media wake-false marker path to the mock OpenAI server, Telegram proof SUT env/config, media lifecycle hook, Mantis prompt, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +60, Tests +149, Docs +12, Other +35. Total +256 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main still reaches the blocked false-return branch after wakeTaskCompletion returns false, and this PR adds a marker hook to force that branch. No completed live Telegram proof has been provided.

target.title: [codex] Add generated media wake proof harness
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

This is a proof-harness follow-up for #97275, #95910, and #86034. The previous Telegram Desktop Mantis harness could not produce live proof for the internal generated-media wake-false path because the local Telegram SUT did not expose `image_generate`, and the mock OpenAI path did not drive a generated-image task into the scheduler-level false-return condition.

## Why This Change Was Made

#97275 is intentionally scoped to the production fix. This PR keeps the proof infrastructure separate so Mantis can later run the same generated-media scenario on baseline and candidate, with the expected visible delta in native Telegram Desktop.

After ClawSweeper review, the proof stimulus now forces the correct layer: the trusted Telegram SUT sets `OPENCLAW_E2E_FORCE_GENERATED_MEDIA_WAKE_FALSE=1`, and only generated image tasks whose prompt/result carries `OPENCLAW_E2E_GENERATED_MEDIA_WAKE_FALSE_PROOF` are converted into a recoverable scheduler-level `generated_media_missing` wake miss. This avoids relying on a normal text completion path that current main can already recover internally.

## User Impact

No normal media delivery behavior changes. The new hook is constrained by both a Mantis-only env var and a dedicated marker:

- enables a mock image-generation model in the Telegram proof SUT
- allows the QA harness to call the loopback mock image endpoint
- adds a dedicated `OPENCLAW_E2E_GENERATED_MEDIA_WAKE_FALSE_PROOF` marker path in the mock OpenAI server
- adds the trusted `OPENCLAW_E2E_FORCE_GENERATED_MEDIA_WAKE_FALSE=1` SUT hook for scheduler-level wake-false proof
- documents the Mantis stimulus and baseline/candidate expectations
- adds tests covering the marker path, proof hook, and prompt/SUT constraints

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts test/scripts/e2e-mock-config-limits.test.ts test/scripts/mantis-telegram-desktop-proof-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
  - passed: 76 tests across 4 files
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts test/scripts/e2e-mock-config-limits.test.ts test/scripts/mantis-telegram-desktop-proof-workflow.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts test/scripts/e2e-mock-config-limits.test.ts test/scripts/mantis-telegram-desktop-proof-workflow.test.ts .github/codex/prompts/mantis-telegram-desktop-proof.md`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`

Note: this PR now supplies a targeted live-proof hook. It still does not claim a completed Telegram Desktop visual proof artifact until Mantis runs successfully with the corrected stimulus.
